title: Add MCP tool annotations (readOnly/destructive/idempotent hints)
source: MCP tool annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint, openWorldHint) — knowledge cutoff Jan 2026; verify at modelcontextprotocol.io
description: The cto-orchestrator MCP server exposes write tools (create_ticket, close_ticket, update_ticket_status, delegate, unity_scan) alongside read tools with no behavioral metadata. MCP tool annotations let clients label read-only vs destructive vs idempotent operations, which improves auto-approval UX, guardrails, and how orchestrating agents reason about side effects before calling.
proposed_changes: In each tool registration in mcp_server.py, add an `annotations` object: readOnlyHint=true for board/list_tickets/get_ticket/project_status/prometheus_status/sleepy_status; destructiveHint=true for close_ticket; idempotentHint=true for update_ticket_status/reserve_files; openWorldHint=true for unity_scan/prometheus_scan (they reach external state). Keep titles human-readable via the `title` annotation.
